response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist software engineer with 15+ years of experience and staff-level tenure at Google and Altom, and his current Google role touches agentic workflows and MCP tooling directly relevant to this position. However, the role requires 8+ years of production AI/ML delivery, and his AI-specific work appears to be only about one year old (April 2025–present). Python — the dominant language for AI engineering — is absent from his listed skills, and there is no explicit mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or enterprise customer-facing technical leadership, all of which are central to this staff-level forward-deployed role.
strengths: Current Google role involves MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure, directly aligning with the job's key technologies
Consistent staff-level engineering track record across Google, Altom, and Meta with demonstrated TypeScript, AWS, and distributed-systems depth
Strong knowledge-sharing and mentoring background evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and open-source library authorship
concerns: Python is absent from the skills list and resume entirely, yet it is the primary language called out in both required and preferred qualifications for AI engineering
No demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, etc.)
Zero evidence of customer-facing forward-deployed or consulting work; the role requires leading technical discovery sessions with enterprise stakeholders

key_factors.factor: Absence of Python and AI depth
MCP and agentic work at Google
